Learning Outcome: Use only CivicActions-approved generative AI tools with IT-managed accounts; never input sensitive or confidential information into AI tools; ensure human oversight for all AI-generated content (verify accuracy, label internally, peer-review before external sharing)

Use GitHub Copilot exclusively for source code creation; carefully review and test AI-generated code for correctness and security; describe AI usage in peer review requests; use other approved tools for non-code technical queries
Comply with client-specific AI policies and guidance; never enter CUI into AI tools; ensure AI restrictions are included in project onboarding and wrapper documents

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Do not use AI tools for pricing or original RFP content generation; AI may assist with analysis, review, and content rewriting

Identify the approved tool categories and licensing requirements: general purpose (Slack Chatbot, Gemini, ChatGPT for Teams), coding (GitHub Copilot), specialist (Grammarly, Paxton AI), service integrations (Zoom, Mural, Confluence/Jira, Greenhouse, Figma), and programmatic APIs (Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI — approval required); know that ChatGPT's Atlas browser is not approved

Training Name: Training 6 — Access Governance, Risk & Supplier Oversight
Learning Outcome: Understand that new AI tools or significant new applications require CTO approval (and client approval for client projects); know that a risk assessment guided by the NIST AI Risk Management Framework is required
Freely explore AI tools and topics for learning purposes, provided only publicly available data is used
